Visit The Serengeti Safari:
One of the most well-liked tourist destinations in Africa is the Serengeti and for good reason. It's a place where you can fully appreciate the grandeur and majesty of nature because of its huge open plains, amazing wildlife, and rich cultural legacy.

Mnemba Island: 
Zanzibar Island is well known as Spice Island due to the farming and processing of aromatic spices like cloves, turmeric, cinnamon, nutmeg, lemongrass, and black pepper. Visit spice plantations to learn about the process and history of these crops, and their medicinal and culinary uses.

Rock restaurant:
The Rock Zanzibar sits on top of a massive boulder in the middle of the Indian Ocean. The 12-table restaurant offers both indoor and outdoor dining, but there's not a bad seat in the house—everywhere you look, you'll be surrounded by jaw-dropping turquoise water.

Prison Island:
The initial idea of building a prison is where the term Prison Island came from. Actually, it was briefly employed as a coral mine and a prison for freed slaves in the 1860s. On the island, no prison facilities were actually constructed; instead, it served as a quarantine station for those with yellow fever.

Zanzibar Scuba diving:
Everyone can find something to enjoy while scuba diving in Zanzibar, including deep dives, macro aficionados, and snorkelers. Zanzibar is more than simply a beach island for relaxation because it has more than 30 dive sites, including reef, wall, and wreck diving.

Paje Beach:
One of Zanzibar's most idealistic, peaceful, distinctive, and beautiful locations is Paje. The ideal white-sand beach is Paje. Locals like seaweed farmers, fishermen who bike to work, and Masaai will be the main beach users.

Watch A Sunset On Kendwa Beach:
There is adequate room for every vacationer in Kendwa because it is a long and wide beach. The water swiftly and steadily gets deeper despite the flat slope. There are no shells, stones, or algae in the shallow water. Tall perennial palms and fine white sand are present along the coast.

videos.transcript: Get ready for an adventure of a lifetime as we take you to the breathtaking island of Zanzibar. Known for its crystal clear waters, white sandy beaches, and lush tropical forests, Zanzibar is a paradise on Earth. From the vibrant culture, delicious seafood, and rich history to the exhilarating water activities, there's something for everyone on this magical island. So, come along with us as we discover the hidden gems, and uncover the secrets of Zanzibar. Number 15, visit the Serengeti Safari. Embark on an epic adventure with a safari in Tanzania's Serengeti National Park. Get ready to witness the majesty of the African wilderness and spot the elusive lion on the prowl. During the wilderbeast migration, you'll have a front row seat to the hunt. The best time to visit is between July and October. See a variety of wild animals including wilderbeast, zebra, leopard, gazelle, giraffe, hyena, elephant, servil, agama lizard, hyrax, secretary bird, rhinoceros, buffalo, cheetah, jackal, and artwolf. This is an unforgettable safari experience you won't want to miss. Number 14. Swim with the dolphins at Nemba Island. Dolphin watching is the ultimate aquatic adventure. Explore the beautiful coral reef and marvel at the variety of colorful fish and marine creatures. Get a chance to witness playful dolphins in their natural habitat. And you can also swim with them. Your guide will do their best to make sure the dolphins are comfortable. The Nemba Atole Marine Reserve on Nemba Island offers a snorkeling paradise with vast corals and rare aquatic life. Explore the spectacular underwater world of bright colors that are home to the green turtles, seahorses, trumpet fish, moray eels, and Napoleon Ras. Snorkeling enthusiasts can visit Watami and West Gardens, while scuba divers can explore Kachuana Reef, the aquarium, and the big wall, all varying from 12 to 50 m in depth. Don't miss out on this incredible opportunity to discover the beauty of Zanzibar's marine life. Number 13, have dinner at the Rock Restaurant. Indulge in a unique dining experience at The Rock Restaurant, one of the most interesting places to eat in Zanzibar. Set upon a rock that becomes an island at high tide, the restaurant is located on the southeast coast not far from some of the island's best beaches. Enjoy delicious seafood while taking in the captivating views from the platform of the rock that rises out from the sand and sea with trees growing on the sides. The Rock Restaurant is the perfect place for a romantic dinner with your partner, offering an unforgettable dining experience with stunning views. Number 12. Discover history and nature at Prison Island. Explore the ruins of the former detention center for slaves and see the famous giant land tortoises. Take a dip in the turquoise waters. Snorkel among vibrant marine life and soak up the sun on the stunning beach. This island, also known as Changangu Island, is located just off the coast of Stonetown, a historic city known for its landmarks and scenic views. It's home to some of the world's oldest turtles, with one of the turtles having a lifespan of over 197 years. The giant tortoises are the main attraction with ages written on their shells, making this island a unique destination and a symbol of the island's rich ecological heritage. Visit the island during the dry season from June to October or December to February for the best experience. Don't miss this opportunity to immerse yourself in history and nature. Number 11, head out to Napenda Sandbank. Escape to Nuca Pinda Sandbank, a snorkeling paradise off the coast of Stonetown. Just a 20-minute boat ride from Stonetown and you'll be transported to a secluded paradise. Once on the sandbank, gear up with provided snorkeling equipment to dive into the breathtaking underwater world. Discover the vibrant coral reefs and an array of colorful fish and sea creatures that thrive in this barrier reef ecosystem. After an exhilarating snorkeling experience, relax on the sandbank and indulge in tropical fruits like banana, pineapple, mangoes, and watermelon. Don't miss out on this unforgettable adventure in Zanzibar. Number 10. Explore Joani National Park. Escape the hustle and bustle of the beach and take a brief trip to the beautiful Joani National Park, the only land-based national park in Zanzibar. Explore the lush forest and discover the unique wildlife that call this place home. The park is open daily from 7:30 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. and includes a small entrance fee and guided tour. Wander along the elevated boardwalk and keep an eye out for the rare red cullibus monkey. You might be surprised by how close they come to you. If you're headed to Paet Beach, consider visiting the park in the morning to avoid the crowds and make the most of your experience. Number nine, feed and swim with turtles at Baraka Natural Aquarium. Dive into a sea of adventure in the north of Zanzibar. Get up close and personal with the majestic sea turtles of Baraka Natural Aquarium. Here, rescued turtles are given a second chance to thrive in their natural habitat. Immerse yourself in the crystal clear waters and swim alongside these magnificent creatures. Don't miss your chance to witness, feed, and swim with these beautiful native animals. Visit Zanzibar's premier turtle sanctuary today. Number eight, visit Stone Town and House of Freddy Mercury. Explore Zanzibar's cultural hub, Stone Town, and visit Freddy Mercury's birthplace on an unforgettable adventure. Stroll through narrow alleyways, bustling markets, and admire ancient architecture. Discover the house where Freddy Mercury was born and walk through rooms filled with the magic of his music. Learn about the legendary rockstar's life and immerse yourself in the rich history and cultural heritage of Stone Town. Don't miss this chance to visit Stone Town in Freddy Mercury's house. Number seven, go scuba diving in Zanzibar. Dive into the crystal clear waters of Zanzibar and discover a world of vibrant marine life and breathtaking coral reefs. Explore the colorful depths of the ocean and swim alongside schools of tropical fish, graceful sea turtles, and playful dolphins. With each descent, you'll be transported to a world of awe and wonder. The warm waters of Zanzibbar make for the perfect scuba diving experience, offering visibility of up to 15 m and water temperatures ranging from 27 C to 30 C. Prepare for an adventure of a lifetime by booking your Zanzibar scuba diving excursion today. Number six, discover the underwater wonderland at Blue Lagoon. Dive into a world of wonder at Blue Lagoon Zanzibar. This snorkeller's paradise boasts vibrant, healthy corals, and an abundance of colorful reef fish. Discover leaf fish, a mantis shrimps, octopi, squids, moray eels, puffer fish, and more. With the highest starfish population, the sight of these brilliant creatures amidst the corals will leave you in awe. Experience the ultimate snorkeling adventure with a traditional wooden boat ride, a local touch that has tourists raving. Get your snorkeling gear ready for the trip of a lifetime at Blue Lagoon's Anzabar. Number five, go sailing on a traditional Dao. Discover the ancient art of traditional Dao ship building in Nunuai, the hub of this unique craft specific to East Africa. Imagine the timber boats drifting across the sea as you explore the ship building regions in the Nunguai village tour. For a truly memorable experience, take a Dao cruise and watch the sunset over the calm Indian Ocean. Don't miss out on this opportunity to venture beyond the lazy beaches and discover the rich culture and history of Nunguay. Number four, have a beach day at Nunguai Beach. Explore the charming village of Nunguay. Located on Zanzibar's northwest coast, this beloved destination is known for its quaint personality, proximity to snow white sand, and its picturesque beaches. Nunguai Beach has previously been rated as one of the best beaches in the world. The sand is as soft as marshmallows and leads to the island's patented blue sea. The water stays shallow for a great distance, allowing you to walk out, sit down on the sand, and watch the world go by. Nanguai remains the heart of ship building on the island, making the surrounding village a delightful place to explore. Learn about the town's boat building heritage. Visit the beach and even join a sunset cruise to complete your Nanguai experience. Number three, kite surf at Paet beach. Unleash your inner adrenaline junkie at Paet beach, a kite surfing paradise located on the southeast coast of Zanzibar. Split between the villages of Buu and Jambiani, this beach offers exceptional kite surfing conditions, and it's quieter than the more popular northern beaches, giving you more room to perfect your skills. With an outer reef that provides a natural lagoon, you can learn to kite surf in calm conditions without the constant threat of barreling waves. The beach also boasts a wide side throughout the year, perfect for zipping along the beach and back again. Beyond the sand, you'll find plenty of schools and rental shops catering to all skill levels and a lively backpacker atmosphere and chill beachside bars on the nearby streets. Number two, watch a sunset on Kendo Beach. Get ready for the ultimate beach vacation at Kendo Beach, one of the best beaches in Zanzibar, located on the northwest coast. Unlike other beaches on the island, Kendo Beach has minimal tide changes, ensuring the golden sand and crystal clear waters are picture perfect all day long. The wide open beach is backed by several resorts, ensuring the sand remains clean, and there's ample space to find your own patch of sand. Not only that, the swimming is calm and colorful with ample opportunities to sport colorful starfish. But the highlight of Kenwa Beach is the spectacular sunsets as the beach faces due west. Enjoy the golden hour and join the fun party atmosphere at the resort and local bars. And don't miss the monthly full moon party at Kendo Rocks Hotel. Number one, unwind and enjoy street food in the Foradani Gardens. Explore the Ford Donni Gardens, a hidden gem nestled among the historical buildings in Zanzibar. Originally built to commemorate Sultan Khalifa in 1936. Now, this local park is a must-sea destination where you can wander through the local market and sample delicious street foods. As the sun sets, the park transforms into a vibrant night market where vendors come together to sell an incredible array of Swahili and Zanzibar delicacies. Indulge in a feast of biryani, mandazi, and mishkaki skewers, and an array of seafood with East African, Persian, and Portuguese influences. Don't miss out on the chance to experience the culinary delights of Zanzibar. So, what are your travel plans for Zanzibar? Let us know in the comments section below. For more travel bucket list videos, like, share, subscribe, and turn on the notifications. See you in the next video. Till then, take care and thanks for watching. [Music]

forget it do yourself a favor and choose other places on the African continent that offer strong and fast internet such as Kenya or South Africa until the government of zanba improves the Wi-Fi situation I was told that starlink is about to be introduced in zanba soon so this might change the situation on the ground but for now zanba is not the best place for people who make their money online and then there are many people who believe that they can come to zanba and look for a job on the ground in the tourism industry let me tell you something unless you have a job offer and you have passed the interview don't come to zanba and look for a job every day I'm reading messages on the Facebook group for zanba residents where people send messages like this you have to understand the chances of getting a job on the ground are very very low of course there are some people who succeeded but you have to understand that your employee needs to pay for your residence permit which is a minimum of $4,000 for two years so that you can work legally in the country I mean ask yourself why would the employee choose you pay all this money for your work and residence permit when there are thousands of qualified locals not just in Tanzania Mainland but also Kenya as a popular place from where employees recruit their workers from so unless you bring in a lot of expertise and knowledge in the tourism industry in the hotel business of course they will choose for the locals whose wages are extremely low compared to what you could get and let me be bloody honest with you but the average wages that are paid to foreign Hotel managers in the big resorts are not that high and if you think you can just work without the proper permits think again because the government has been cracking down on unregistered workers next let's talk about education and schools which is important for parents who seek to settle on the island of zanba with their children if you have small children toddlers babies zanba is a beautiful place to raise your little child but if it reaches a school age be prepared to pay a lot of money for their International School there is not much competition and accordingly The Intern schools that are available charge extremely high prices that are in my opinion not justified for the education and the extracurricular activities which they offer so be prepared to pay up to 600 700 up to, us per month of course there are international schools that even charge higher fees in other places on the continent like here in Nairobi for instance there are international schools that charge up to 2 ,000 3,000 us per month what I'm trying to say is that the extremely high school fees that international schools in zanba charge are not justified no way not for what these schools offer in terms of education and extracurricular activities next let's talk about the challenges of starting a business most people who want to settle on the island of Zanzibar want to invest in the tourism industry dream of buying a piece of land Building beautiful properties which they can then rent out via short-term rental platforms like booking.com or Airbnb if that is you go then please be prepared for a lot of bureaucracy unnecessary fees and extreme delays many people who visit zanba for vacation return to their home countries with the goal of saving money and then buying a plot in zanba and starting a new life there and that is mainly because they have been misguided by locals who were offering ing them uh affordable and even cheap plots but they are not aware of the process that lies in front of them they are not aware of the fact that you need to register a company to be able to lease the land from the government they think they can just buy a piece of land and own it but the reality is that nobody can own land in zanba all land belongs to the government you can only lease a piece of land from the government and that is usually 33 years of course you can extend it but the process to get there is not easy and that's what many people are not prepared for you need to register a company and to register a company you need to either work with a local partner who gets 70% of the shares and if you want to become the sole shareholder of that company you need to have a minimum investment capital of $2 million us I think was my last information I heard this has changed again you know also be prepared that laws are changing constantly besides that there are so many people who get scammed because they didn't do their due diligence when buying a piece of land I heard of countless stories from people who were being scammed who lost their land who lost the property so before even thinking of buying a piece on land on the island of zanba get some legal advice last but not least before moving to Zanzibar you need to be aware of the language integration and Community Dynamics living in Zanzibar means embracing a new culture and integration is key like in any other country most zanzibari are Muslim and Swahili is the local language spoken on the island learning Swahili and understanding local Traditions is not just polite it's essential for building trust and respect in the community this isn't just about adjusting to the lifestyle it's about forming meaningful connections but here is the challenge many experts stick to their own communities and create a divide between themselves and the locals so I want you to ask yourself are you ready to step out of the expert bubble adapting to the culture whether it's dressing modestly or understanding local Traditions is a sign of respect moving to and living in Zanzibar and any other place in Africa in my opinion should be about contributing to the community and not just recreating a western lifestyle in Paradise building meaningful relationships with locals takes effort but it's incredibly rewarding in my opinion and gives you a deeper and more authentic experience true integration and meaningful relationships with the locals will make your life on the island far richer than you could have ever imagined if you're considering of starting a business on the island of zanba you should definitely watch the next video and I look forward to see you next time bye

videos.transcript: Are you looking to visit the beautiful tropica island of Zanzibar? Then this video is perfect for you as we have prepared a detailed guide on how to spend 7 days in Zanzibar to make the most of your time there. Take a moment to give this video a like and subscribe to our channel for more beautiful travel destinations and interesting lists. Make sure to watch till the end so you don't miss out on all the essential tips and recommendations we give you throughout this video. And let's dive right in. Welcome to Zanzibar. Once you land in Stonetown, the island's historic heart, your adventure begins. Start by picking up a rental car. Having your own transport will make it easy to explore Zanzibar's treasures at your own pace. After settling into your hotel, head out to the lively Stone Town Prominard for your first taste of Zanzibar's energy. The evening is perfect for a leisurely stroll along the seafront, where you'll catch breathtaking views of the Indian Ocean as Dows sail across the horizon. This is also the ideal spot to dive into local culture. For dinner, try a Zanzibari favorite like octopus curry or the flavorful Swahili biryani at a waterfront restaurant. The fresh seafood and fragrant spices will be your introduction to the island's culinary magic. End your day with the ocean breeze and the city's nightife, setting the stage for an incredible week ahead. Let's continue with day two. Today is all about soaking in the vibrant history and culture of Stonetown. Begin your day by wandering through its narrow, winding streets, where every corner reveals something new. Head to the old fort, a historic structure that stands as a testament to Zanzibar's rich past. Next, make your way to the Sultan's Palace Museum for a glimpse into Zanzibar's royal heritage. You'll also want to visit the Anglican Cathedral and the former slave market. For lunch, stop by a local cafe and enjoy a Zanzibar pizza, a unique and flavorful street food stuffed with everything from meat to vegetables and cheese. Afterward, lose yourself in the Darajani market where you can shop for spices, crafts, and souvenirs. As evening approaches, head to the Foradani Gardens for the famous night food market. Here you can indulge in a variety of local dishes from fresh seafood skewers to sugarcane juice while enjoying the lively atmosphere by the water. Day three takes you into the heart of Zanzibar's natural beauty. Start your morning with a drive to Jazani Forest, the island's only national park. Here you'll walk through lush greenery and encounter Zanzibar's famous red colibus monkeys, a rare and endangered species. After exploring the forest, head to the nearby Jazani sea turtle sanctuary. This hidden gem is dedicated to the conservation of sea turtles, offering you a chance to see these gentle creatures up close and learn about the local efforts to protect them. For lunch, grab a bite at a nearby local ery where you can try dishes like Samaki Wakupaka, fish in coconut sauce. In the afternoon, make your way to Muni Beach. Known for its white sands and crystal clear waters, this secluded beach is the perfect place to unwind. Wrap up the day with a dinner at one of the beachfront restaurants, where you can enjoy grilled seafood while watching the sun dip below the horizon. Your fourth day is all about unique experiences that showcase Zanzibar's wildlife and natural wonders. Start your day with a visit to Zala Park, a lesserknown yet fascinating spot. After Zala Park, it's time to head to Kuza Cave. This stunning limestone cave is located in Jambiani, and its crystal clearar freshwater pool is perfect for a refreshing swim. The natural beauty of Kuza Cave combined with the tranquility of its surroundings makes it a serene escape from the busier tourist areas. For lunch, stop at a nearby restaurant in Jambiani Village where you can try Mishkaki, Zanzibar's version of grilled skewers served with a side of spiced rice or chapati. In the afternoon, enjoy more time exploring the village or simply relax by the beach. Jambiani is known for its laid-back vibe and long stretches of sandy coastline. You can take a leisurely walk along the shore or engage with the locals to learn more about traditional Zanzibari life. For dinner, try a local dish like pilao, a flavorful spiced rice paired with fresh seafood or chicken. On day five, get ready for a day of sun, sea, and incredible marine life. Begin your journey with a drive up to Nongui Beach, located on Zanzibar's northern tip. Nungui is famous for its pristine white sand and turquoise waters, making it one of the island's top beach destinations. Spend your morning lounging by the sea, swimming in the crystal clear waters. For lunch, enjoy a meal at one of the many beachside restaurants. You can savor grilled seafood, especially Zanzibar's fresh catch of the day. In the afternoon, visit Baraka Natural Aquarium where you'll have the chance to swim with green sea turtles in a natural lagoon. This sanctuary provides a safe haven for injured turtles, and visitors can interact with these majestic creatures up close. After your time at the aquarium, head back to Nongi Beach for more relaxation. As the day winds down, treat yourself to a delicious dinner at a beachfront restaurant. For day six, prepare for a day of underwater adventure as you set out for Member Island, one of Zanzibar's top snorkeling and diving spots. Start the morning by joining a snorkeling tour that takes you to the waters surrounding the private Man Island. While you can't step foot on the island itself, the surrounding coral reefs are what make this trip special. Here you'll discover a vibrant marine ecosystem teameming with colorful fish, dolphins, and if you're lucky, even sea turtles. Spend the morning snorkeling in the crystal clearar waters where you'll feel like you're swimming in an aquarium. The stunning coral formations and rich marine life make it one of the best underwater experiences Zanzibar has to offer. If you're a diving enthusiast, you can also opt for a scuba diving trip to explore deeper parts of the reef. For dinner, return to the mainland and treat yourself to a beachfront meal at a nearby restaurant. For your final day in Zanzibar, you have two incredible options to choose from, depending on how you want to spend your time. If you're looking to unwind after an adventurous week, head to Tumbatu Island. Known for its seclusion and tranquility, Tumbatu is a perfect spot to disconnect from the hustle and bustle. If you prefer to immerse yourself in local culture, Jambiani Village offers a more active experience. Start your day with a visit to the fisherman's village where you can observe daily life by locals. Take a walk along the long stretch of beach or visit a local artisan shop to pick up some handmade souvenirs. End your trip with a memorable dinner at a beachfront restaurant, indulging in more swuahili flavors like pilao, spiced rice, or a grilled seafood feast. As the sun sets on your last day, you'll look back on a week filled with incredible experiences from historic towns to serene beaches. And there you have it, fellow travelers. Next, go check out our Zanzibar travel guide. If you enjoyed this video, don't forget to like, subscribe, and hit that notification bell to stay updated with our latest adventures. Until then, happy travels.

videos.transcript: If you're planning a trip to Zanzibar and you don't know where to stay, this video is going to save you hours and hours of research. I know that finding your ideal holiday location can be challenging in Zanzibar. So, in this video, I'll break down the best areas to stay, the top resorts and hotels in each area, whether you're splurging on a luxury holiday or you're a budget backpacker. And I'll also tell you what to do in each area, and the top tours and activities in each place. If you're new here, hi, my name is Laa, and I've spent the past few years traveling all over the world to places like Kenya, Pakistan, France, Switzerland, and so much more. I also spent a few months living in Zanzibar earlier this year, and I've traveled quite a lot all over Tanzania, so this is a country that I know particularly well. In this channel, I typically share lots of travel guides, travel vlogs, and also hidden gems. So, if that's something you like, make sure you subscribe for more. And now let's go back into the video. Let's start with the north of the island and its most famous beach, Nangui. So Nangui used to be a small fisherman's village a few decades ago, but now it's become Zanzibar's most famous beach destination. It's particularly well known for its sunsets and its vibrant beach life. And this area is ideal if you want to mix beach days with really great night life. Like pretty much all beaches in Zanzibar, Nangui is very well known for its beautiful turquoise water, its white sand, and its towering palm trees. But the difference with Nongi is that the tide stays out less here. So swimming is great at any time of day, which makes it a preferred destination for a lot of travelers. There are also lots of activities to do in and around Nongi. So sunset cruises are particularly popular and also snorkeling and diving. So Zanzibar in general has really great snorkeling and diving and you can do these activities almost anywhere. So I will only mention these in places where I think that they're particularly good and Nangui is one of those places. Nangu is also very well known for its turtle sanctuary. It's called the Baraka Natural Aquarium if I'm not mistaken and you can see it all over Instagram. It's that place where people go into a little lagoon and swim with sea turtles. And you can also do a day trip to Tumbatu Island. So, Tombatu Island is a secluded, culturally rich island with really, really great coral reefs. So, again, fantastic for snorkeling and diving. That is not a Nongi per se. It's more of a day trip or a half-day trip, but it's still one that I highly recommend and that not a lot of people know about. Lastly, Nongi has a great horse center. It's called the Zanzibar Horse Club. And here you can go into the beach with horses, into the beautiful water, whether you're an experienced horse rider or you're just a beginner. Even if you've never horse ridden before, you can do a little tour here. And they have tours available for all levels. So this is something that I also highly recommend. As for where to stay in Nongi, you've got accommodation options for all budget. So, if you're on a budget, if you don't want to spend a lot of money, I would recommend that you stay at Makofi Guest House, which is a backpacker friendly hostel with a social vibe. If your budget is more mid-range, then Baraka Beach Bungalows is a great option for you. It's simple, it's got a beachfront, and it's quite comfortable. And if you're looking for luxury, then the Z Hotel is a boutique luxury hotel right in front of the beach and that is perfect for you. Moving on to Kenda. So Kentw is a beach destination just south of Nongui and it used to be known as kind of a hidden German Zansbar. People tend to say that Nongui is a lot busier with tourists and Kenda was the place where you wouldn't find any tourists and it would be a lot quieter and a lot more laid-back. And I do think this used to be true, but because Zanspar's tourism has grown so much in the past couple of years, I don't think that Kendo is a hidden gem anymore. I would call it quite touristic, but it is true that it does have a more laid-back vibe than Nongui and it is a bit quieter. So, if you don't like tourist crowds or super touristic places, then Kenwa might be a slightly better option for you. Kenwa is most well known for its full moon party. So, this is a monthly party, very, very popular with tourists and locals in Zanzibar. So, it is definitely a great place for night life, but it also has a beautiful stretch of beach where you can chill and just have some really nice relaxing beach days. So, it's a great place to combine those two things, like the relaxing aspect of the beach, but also the partying aspect of it. I wouldn't say that Kendo is really a budget destination. There aren't many budget options. So, when it comes to choosing a place to stay, if you have a mid-range luxury, you can stay at Kendo Rocks, which is actually the host of the Full Moon Party. It's right on the beach. And again, this place is known for night life. And if you have more of a luxury stay in mind, then Gold Santar Beach House is an upscale resort with fine dining and a spa, and this is the perfect place for you. Heading on to the east coast, let's talk about Baj. So Baja is located in the southeastern region of the island. And I would say that along with Nangui, this is the most famous and most popular beach destination with tourists in Zanzibar. Baj is also a hot spot for backpackers. It's much more budget friendly and it's also Zanzibar's kite surfing capital. So, Baj has very very consistent winds and it is definitely the best place on the island to practice this sport and to learn kite surfing or enjoy it if you already know how to kite surf. Obviously, the top thing that you can do in Badger is kite surf and relax on the beach, walk on the beach. There's also lots of beach bars. This place is perfect for budget travelers, digital nomads, and you can also do DAO sunset cruises here. When it comes to where to stay, if you're a budget backpacker, then Drifter's Backpackers is a popular hostel with really social vibes. If you've got more of a mid-range budget, then Ananda Beach Hotel is relaxed, clean, and right on the beach. And if you're going for luxury, I generally wouldn't recommend Pa for you, but you can also find luxury in Page. Kissa on the beach is really elegant. They have really elegant villas with ocean views, and you might like this place. I actually have a travel vlog about Bajger if you're interested in watching that. And because I spent a few months living in Zanzibar and I've spent so much time traveling around Zanzibar and Tanzania, I have two playlists, a Zanzibar playlist and a Tanzania playlist with so many videos about this country. I have so many travel vlogs about places like the Enorangoro, Serenetti, Arouchia. I also have vlogs about Safari Blue in Zanzibar, Stone Town, lots of other beach destinations, and so much more. And I also have a ton of travel guides. So, all of the information that you could possibly want or need about traveling to Tanzania or Zanzibar is in those playlists. So, I will link them in the description down below, but I would definitely check them out if I were you. And if you're planning a trip to Zanzibar, Tanzania, definitely definitely check those out because they have so much useful info and they will save you so much time when researching. Right next to Baja, you can find Jambiani. So, Jambiani used to be a little fisher village that was unaccessible to tourists for a very long time. So, it offers a quieter, more authentic village feel. It's perfect for slow travelers and those really looking to soak in the local culture. So this is a place that definitely offers a lot more cultural experience while still having those beautiful, beautiful beach vibes. The top activities that you can do here are village walks. You can also do seaweed farming tours. And these two activities really offer an insight into what local life is like in Zanzibar and particularly life in the beach and rural life. You can also do reef snorkeling and scuba diving here. There's a beautiful blue lagoon in the ocean which is perfect for snorkeling and diving. And there's also a diving school here called Poly Polola Divers. Kuza Cave is also nearby and this is a beautiful freshwater cave with lovely turquoise water. It's ideal for a swim and this is definitely a place that you should add to your bucket list when you're in Zanzibar. Much like Baj Jambiani is a place for budget and mid-range travelers. If you want luxury, you can find it here, but I honestly would recommend that you go somewhere else if you're looking for luxury. There's no shortage of luxury accommodation in Zanzibar. So just Jambiani and budget are just not the best places for it. But if you're on a budget, I recommend that you stay at a place called Blue Earth Bungalows, which are beachfront bungalows with a rustic charm. videos.transcript: Welcome back to Explore in Style, your ultimate guide to luxury travel with a touch of sophistication. Today, we're diving into the enchanting island of Zanzavar, a tropical paradise known for its white sandy beaches, rich history, and vibrant culture. But as with any destination, there are risks and scams that can turn your dream vacation into a nightmare. In this video, we'll reveal what not to do in Zanzibar this summer 2025, so you can enjoy your trip without any hiccups. From accommodation and dining to transportation and shopping, we've got you covered. Let's get started. But before we dive in, make sure to smash that like button and subscribe to the channel so you never miss out on our upcoming videos. Now, let's get started. Accommodation. Zanzavar offers a wide range of accommodations from luxury resorts to budgetfriendly guest houses. However, one of the most common scams revolves around booking your stay. Picture this. You're scrolling through a popular booking platform like Booking.com or Airbnb and you stumble upon a stunning beachfront villa at an unbelievably low price. The photos are breathtaking, the reviews are glowing, and you're tempted to book it immediately. But hold on. Scammers often create fake listings using stolen photos and fabricated reviews to lure unsuspecting travelers. You arrive at the property only to find it doesn't exist or looks nothing like the pictures. To avoid this, always book through reputable platforms and doublech checkck the property's legitimacy. Look for verified reviews. Contact the host directly. And if something feels off, trust your instincts. Don't let the allure of a cheap deal ruin your trip before it even begins. dining. Zanzavar is a food lover's paradise with its fresh seafood, exotic spices, and Swahili inspired dishes. However, there are a few pitfalls to avoid when it comes to dining on the island. First, be cautious of street food if you have a sensitive stomach. While it's tempting to try local delicacies from street vendors, hygiene standards can vary. Stick to reputable restaurants or hotels for meals, especially if you're not used to the local cuisine. Another issue is the special catch of the day scam. You're offered a freshly caught fish, but the price is not discussed upfront. After your meal, you're presented with an exorbitant bill, leaving you with no choice but to pay. To avoid this, always ask for prices before ordering, especially for seafood. Stick to reputable restaurants, and if you're unsure, ask your hotel or a trusted local for recommendations. Lastly, don't miss out on the island's fine dining experiences. Restaurants like The Rock and Emerson Spice offer not only delicious food, but also breathtaking views and unforgettable ambiencece. Experiences. Zanzibar is known for its incredible experiences. From snorkeling in the crystal clear waters of Menatol to exploring the island's spice farms. However, not all tour operators are created equal. Some may offer tours at rock bottom prices only to cut corners or exploit both travelers and local communities. For example, you might book a dolphin watching tour only to find yourself on an overcrowded boat chasing dolphins in a way that stresses the animals. Or you might visit a spice farm and be pressured into buying overpriced products. To avoid these issues, research tour operators thoroughly. Look for companies with positive reviews, ethical practices, and transparent pricing. Remember, if a deal seems too good to be true, it probably is. Shopping. Zanzibar's markets are a treasure trove of unique souvenirs, from handcrafted jewelry to aromatic spices. But shopping here can be a double-edged sword. One common scam involves aggressive vendors who use high pressure sales tactics to get you to buy overpriced items. For instance, you might be offered a free sample of a spice or piece of jewelry only to be guilted into making a purchase. Another tactic is the bait and switch where you're shown a highquality item, but the one you receive is of inferior quality. To navigate these situations, always negotiate politely, but firmly. Don't feel pressured to buy something you don't want, and be wary of deals that seem too good to be true. Transportation. Getting around Zanzavar can be an adventure, but it's important to choose your transportation wisely. One mistake to avoid is hiring unlicensed taxis or drivers. Always use reputable taxi services like Xan Taxi or ask your hotel to arrange transportation for you. Another thing to watch out for is overcharging. Agree on a fair before getting into a taxi or boarding a boat to avoid surprises at the end of your journey. If you're planning to rent a car, make sure to book through a trusted rental company like Avis or Europe Car. Be aware that driving in Zanzibar can be challenging due to narrow roads and unpredictable traffic. So consider hiring a driver if you're not comfortable behind the wheel. Don't forget to enjoy the island's traditional modes of transportation like the iconic thou. Just make sure to book through a licensed operator for safety and reliability. And there you have it, our guide on what not to do in Zanzibar this summer 2025. By avoiding these common mistakes and scams, you can focus on enjoying the island's beauty, culture, and luxury. Remember to book through trusted platforms, stay vigilant, and always prioritize your safety. Zanzavar is a destination like no other. And with the right precautions, your trip will be nothing short of extraordinary. So, pack your bags, embrace the island vibes, and get ready to explore in style. If you found this video helpful, don't forget to give it a thumbs up, and subscribe to Explore in Style for more luxury travel tips and guides. Have you been to Zanzibar? Share your experiences and tips in the comments below. We'd love to hear from you. And if you're planning your trip, check out the links in the description for trusted booking platforms, tour operators, and more. Until next time, keep exploring in style. See you soon.
